entry.title: Re: Release procedure configuration using standard workfl...
entry.content: Thanks for your inputs. Is there any other standard way we can reduce this effort? How about the second option i mentioned?

entry.title: Re: Where is the Raylightsamples.lcmbiar file in SAP BO 4.3
entry.content:
No, this has nothing to do with RESTful API, what you are using here is BI Java SDK.
and the LCMBIAR contains the sample report and universes used as tests in the sample Java program.



See the Java code in the src directory, e.g. the file "ChangeSourceSample.java", which shows how to change the source universe in the document.
...

Java SDK Guides
BI Semantic Layer Java SDK Developer Guide
Business Intelligence Platform Java SDK Developer Guide

RESTful API
SAP BusinessObjects RESTful Web Service SDK User Guide for Web Intelligence and the BI Semantic Layer
Business Intelligence Platform RESTful Web Service Developer Guide

entry.title: Cannot find type "MAINT_TEAM" in RSH app
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using S/4 HANA 2022 version. I am trying to create and WC teams and responsibility and I want to use type "MAINT_TEAM"(Maintenance Work Center Team) but this option is not available in the dropdown list. This value should be available as a standard but I cannot find any. 

How do I get this value??

Thanks,

Shweta Gupta

entry.title: Re: Making employees recently increased in EC ineligible in compensation worksheets
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec,

Thank you so much for your quick response and for documenting the solution! 😊

entry.title: How to Notify me when password expired
entry.content:
Hello community , 

i need to  know if i can notify a user that his code is expiring in "N" days. (N=5 days for example)

I want to notify a user when Login In to the system if the password expiring in 5 Days , to Change it.

Can i do this ?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Release procedure configuration using standard workfl...
entry.content: Are you talking about 50 release codes? So you want to configure them and not create a prerequisite for each of them, so that theoretically a specific PR can be released by each code. Then you want to create 50 roles, each for a different code. But then how would you manage the workflow notifications? In the PR there is a plant in a workflow table, but in the PO we don't have that. Also, if you configure 50 release codes, you will see them all in every single document. This can be confusing. The release code is usually a release level and is not used to differentiate between characteristics. For this we have the release groups.

entry.title: Re: ABAP & http, why can’t we just have something nice?
entry.content:
Hi,

As already announced through the roadmap item

 Integration Services | SAP Help Portal "Outbound HTTP/RFC: Deployment-Independent Code" we have released the Communication Targets for HTTP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.

A Communication Target is a new development object that enables content separation of application destinations. As soon as Communication Targets are also available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA  the same concept can be applied in all deployments for outbound HTTP communication.

For more information you can refer to the 2408 release note ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse: Communication Targets for HTTP Outbound Communication at What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA Cloud | SAP Help Portal and the documentation HTTP Communication via Communication Targets | SAP Help Portal.

Regards

Tatjana Pfeifer

entry.title: Re: India TCS Form 27EQ - Remmitance Challan Issue
entry.content:
Dear Moni123, can you please have a look at these resources in the screenshot, the screenshot is one paragraph of a larger post in the community: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/gst-india-tcs-solution/ba-p/13499648#common

Please let me know whether resolved your issue

Regards,

Axel

entry.title: Re: F110 change posting date
entry.content:
https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3353346

3353346 said that it is not possible to change posting date after executing the payment proposal.

entry.title: Batch Validation/check during Sales Delivery
entry.content:
Hi,

We have Requirement of that Expired batch and batch with shelf life less than 1 year should not pick during the batch determiantion in Sales Delivery.

How we can achive this in standard of public cloud?

entry.title: Re: Issue in delivery from project stock
entry.content: can we upload the note here

entry.title: Reverse kitting without BOM gives error - Wrong stock type
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts, 

We are setting up the VAS Reverse kitting process in our factory and the system is giving a weird error.

The process starts with a production order in which several products are assembled with some packagings to build a new product (we call it box).  Then, for some customers, this box has to be disassembled logically in the system as they ordered the products individually on the sales order (but requested the packaging task). So the users disassemble the box into the products and create pick-HUs for every box HU that was produced earlier. Then the Pick-HUs are shipped.

 For this second part of the process, we would like to implement reverse kitting, but when trying to create the VAS order without BOM (because the BOM for the box has assembly products and we cannot use it for reverse kitting without implementing a BADI to select the right BOM) the system shows an error related to stock type that makes no sense.

The customizing for VAS does not have F1 as stock type, it has I2.

Settings for order type for reverse kitting.

 

Also, we don't have Availability group 001 (that has stock type F1)  assigned to any EWM connected Storage location

Can you please guide me on what could be wrong? Why is the system trying to use F1 instead of I2 stock?

Thank you in advance for your answers.

Regards

Iris Ladera

entry.title: Re: Release procedure configuration using standard workfl...
entry.content: The plant in the workflow table is only for PR and not PO. It is only used in PR release strategy if configured item wise release and not overall release. As a standard solution I understand it is feasible to create 50 different release groups and not release codes. However, my question was can we use option 2 using the customer exit to simplify instead of creating 50 different groups and strategies.

entry.title: Re: Can't found app "Manage Production Supply Areas"
entry.content:
Hi @Enda 

unfortunately these scope items are not available for our country version (Croatia). How can we proceed further?

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Moving average Price History in s4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hello, your question has been forwarded to the experts. We hope to provide an answer soon. Kind regards, Jessica

entry.title: Re: Unable to convert an SAP Script as a PDF and send it...
entry.content: Thanks for the feedback!

entry.title: Re: Document number CO_BELNR to RPSCO
entry.content: Thank for your reply. I have to create a view for the planning data which was entered via CJR2 transaction and it is visible in CJI4 report as business transaction RKP1. I can't see RKP1 business transaction when go to COEP. I tried to find the values in COKP but there I could not find any either. Would you have any other suggestion where I could find planned figures as well as document numbers numbers?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content: If you just want to GET a file through HTTPS, maybe you can run through RFC Destination "SAPHTTP" (via the HTTP Client of the user's laptop) instead of "SAPHTTPA" (via the HTTP Client of the application server). Or create a program in any language but ABAP, on the user's laptop, and run it via cl_gui_frontend_services=>execute.

entry.title: Re: Can Anyone tell me how to use this following BAPI to create multiple inforecords?
entry.content: Please format your code using </> so that it can be read easily. https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-code-in-sap-community-gt-2024/qaq-p/13684774

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I’m Ahmed, and I’m excited to be part of the SAP Community Beginner Corner! I specialize in SAP training and implementation services across Africa and the Middle East. My goal is to help businesses effectively adopt and utilize SAP solutions. Now that I’m part of the Beginner Corner, I’m eager to connect with the community, learn from experienced SAP professionals, and contribute as I begin my journey with SAP. Feel free to send me a private message—I’m looking forward to connecting!

Best regards,

entry.title: Report on supplier turnover per year
entry.content:
Hello everyone ,

we would like to see the standard sap report on supplier turnover per year.

Surely there will be something ready in standard SAP , do you know how I can take this Report ?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Driving Innovation in Utility Security with SAP and Intel Collaboration
entry.content:
In an era where utilities are becoming increasingly reliant on edge computing and IoT devices, ensuring robust security across both physical and cyber domains has never been more critical. The convergence of these domains presents unique challenges that require innovative solutions, and that’s precisely where SAP’s collaboration with Intel comes into play.

As part of our ongoing commitment to delivering cutting-edge technology to SAP customers, SAP is thrilled to announce that Intel will be joining us at our upcoming SAP4U to showcase an exciting new development in Cyber and Physical Security AI at the Edge.

Enhancing Security for Utilities: A Multi-Vector Approach

Utilities today are facing unprecedented challenges in securing their infrastructure. Traditional security measures, which often focus separately on either cyber or physical threats, are no longer sufficient. Utilities require a method to integrate visual identification with physical and cyber security protocols, which is precisely the role of the edge-native edge security solution, Threat Sense at the Edge. This collaborative solution from SAP, Intel, and Alert Enterprise brings together IoT data, utility data, and signals from cameras and other physical environments.

Threat Sense at the Edge, utilizing the Intel Tiber Edge Platform, represents a significant advancement in the way utilities can manage security. By connecting previously siloed data sources, we’re able to bring new value to the edge of the utility environment. This innovation enables the simulation of multi-vector attack incidents, offering a comprehensive view of threats that span both physical and cyber domains. 

At our upcoming SAP4U, attendees  will have the opportunity to learn more about how this integrated approach can simulate a multi-vector attack, helping utilities to better understand the complexities of modern security threats and how to effectively counter them.

Bridging the Gap with Cutting-Edge Collaboration

Threat Sense at the Edge is more than just a technological advancement; it’s a testament to the power of collaboration. By bringing together the expertise of Intel, AlertEnterprise, and SAP, we’ve developed a comprehensive security solution that is tailored to meet the unique challenges of edge computing.

This collaboration highlights the shared commitment to addressing the security concerns associated with edge computing. As businesses continue to expand their networks of edge devices, the need for robust, scalable security solutions has never been greater. The approach ensures that businesses can effectively manage these expanding networks, while also providing the necessary tools to mitigate risks across multiple threat vectors.

The intersection of cyber and physical threats at the edge necessitates a novel, unified security strategy. Intel's partnership and the Threat Sense at the Edge solution are setting the stage for a new generation of indispensable security solutions for the utilities sector.

We invite you to join us at the upcoming SAP4U to learn more about how this collaboration is driving innovation and providing utilities with the tools they need to secure their operations in an increasingly complex threat landscape. This is an opportunity to see the future of security in action and to understand how our solutions can help protect your business from the threats of tomorrow.

Intel will also be delivering a session in the Innovation Lounge focusing on the Open Platform for Enterprise AI. OPEA aims to accelerate secure, cost-effective generative AI (GenAI) deployments for businesses by driving interoperability across a diverse and heterogeneous ecosystem, starting with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).  Both Intel and SAP are contributors to the development and standardization of enterprise-grade RAG AI. Intel’s session, Open Platform for Enterprise AI (OPEA) – A Flexible Open Generative AI Platform, will take place on Wednesday, September 11, 2024, 2:05 pm – 2:45 pm and will be presented by Balaji Srinivasan, Global Business Development Manager, AI Center of Excellence, Intel.

entry.title: Re: Question about profit center consolidation in Group R...
entry.content:
Yes, partner dimensions are a prerequisite to get meaningful elimination results, especially if customers want to have the eliminations appear on the correct hierarchy level where entity and partner meet first. If only top-side eliminations are required, customers may explore to work with a "dummy partner".

On partner profit center derivation in accounting I am not the expert. Maybe someone else could contribute?

entry.content:
Hello there,

I am trying the same scenario of DEEP POST operation using Maintenance Bill of Materials OData V2 ( I could not find V4 version for this API ). But I can see the link which you mentioned for DEEP POST is for OData V4 version and your code uses Version V2 proxy.

entry.title: Database multitenant instance installation for Solution manager 7.2
entry.content:
We are trying to install new Solution manager 7.2(ABAP and JAVA apps separately) on Oracle19c database. Can we go with separate DB for ABAP and JAVA systems or can we go with Oracle multitenant instance installation ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Database multitenant instance installation for Soluti...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Both the standalone Oracle or multitenant Oracle are supported for SAP Solution Manager installation. Please consider the SOLMAN ABAP and JAVA two standalone but being used together systems. 

Attaching the user guide for solman installation on Oracle & RHEL.
ABAP
https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/4b99f675d74f4990b75a8630869a0cd2/9420dabb130e4ae1996b3f39e202cc6e.html?version=CURRENT_VERSION&q=multitenant%20 

JAVA
https://help.sap.com/docs/PRODUCT_ID/08baebca0fef470389e4a4ebcb46b879/9420dabb130e4ae1996b3f39e202cc6e.html?state=PRODUCTION&version=CURRENT_VERSION&locale=en-US 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: How to create Odata connection from BTP instance
entry.content:
I'm trying to connect an OData service with a BTP instance to pull data from SAP Cloud ALM into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). However, I'm encountering issues during the connection setup, particularly with identifying and configuring the necessary OAuth2 details like the Token URL and Data Service URL.

Has anyone successfully connected an OData service for SAP Cloud ALM data in BTP? If so, could you share the steps or best practices for setting up this connection? Any insights on troubleshooting common connection issues would also be highly appreciated

entry.title: Re: Deleted Payment Draft Change Log
entry.content: The deletion including delete of the record in the database table. However you can try use stored procedure to capture the delete event and store in your own SQL table. https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sbo-sp-transactionnotification/ba-p/13148637

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI MFA with SAP Secure Login Service and Microsoft Entra ID
entry.content:
Hi Sridhar,

this approach can be combined with any identity management or access governance solution. Identity management can help to automate the creation of the user accounts in the identity provider and the ABAP systems, and set the correct user mapping in ABAP. This data will then be used during authentication.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: How to block supplier of service employee
entry.content:
Hi. I need your help. 

I would like to know how to block supplier of service employee.

I have ticked all the block indicators of the supplier in Maintain Business Partner app. But it still can be registered as a contract supplier for a service employee.

entry.title: Re: How to change more than one glossar entry?
entry.content:
Hello Merle

I understand you're referring to the "Categories" in our dictionary, which serve as a reference source for specific terms, known as dictionary entries.

In SAP Signavio, the dictionary is a key component within the central object management repository. Once an item is created under a specific category in the dictionary, it becomes available across all your diagrams.

Please let me know if this clarifies your question. If you have any further inquiries, feel free to email us at signavio_success@sap.com.

entry.title: How to convert xml to table, using xml transformation, at utf-8 format.
entry.content:
Hi, experts.

How to convert xml to table, using xml transformation, at utf-8 format?
i'm calling an xml transformation, but cyrillic values are not readable, although it's readable at xml_final.

 

      CALL TRANSFORMATION (lv_trans_name)
      SOURCE XML xml_final
      RESULT root = it_tab.

entry.title: Re: Single Signon 3.0 settings for Kerberos login using a...
entry.content:
Hi,

have you looked at the ServerSessionKeyMode SNC parameter? This creates a temporary token for the duration of the session so that new SAP GUI modes for the same connection do not require re-authentication. It works in SAP Single Sign-On but also in it's successor product, the SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI. 

You will find the documentation at https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP%20SECURE%20LOGIN%20SERVICE/c35917ca71e941c5a97a11d2c55dcacd/3c815833c6244f4fa1bd03e536c5c5e8.html 

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: ST05 Error "For SQL Statements with an ITAB, the Planviz is not supported"
entry.content:
Hi All,
We wanted to analyze the performance of below select query and we are on S4HANA OP2020 system.

SELECT
a~matnr,
a~bwkey,
a~bwtar,
a~lbkum,
a~salk3,
a~verpr,
a~zkprs,
a~timestamp
FROM mbew AS a
WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT matnr, werks FROM @LT_material AS b WHERE matnr = a~matnr ) AND bwkey IN @LT_t001w_rg AND bwtar IN @LT_bwtar
INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mbew_tabn5).

We captured ST05 trace for above SELECT query also and when we are trying to download the plan viz file it gives error like "For SQL Statements with an ITAB, the Planviz is not supported"(PFB screenshot).

It is happening because we are using @LT_material internal table in subquery.We also tried with innerjoin to @LT_material and it give same error.  So how in such cases we can get planviz file? 
Any suggestions?

 



Regards,
Prince

entry.title: Re: Asking and Answering Questions in the Community After Migration
entry.content:
what is cross tab and non aggregated tab in story reports table. In which scenario's do we use it ?

 

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition – the Way Ahead
entry.content:
Today, SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition is available in the Cloud Platform, enterprise agreement environment (CPEA).   

How is SAP Analytics Cloud, public edition different from SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition? 

The current service for SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition has limited BI functionality and is priced per tenant.  It is limited to one HANA Cloud data source and 150 simultaneous users.  The full-use services for SAP Analytics Cloud, public edition will include full BI and Planning functionality and be priced on a user metric, just as the subscription version of SAP Analytics Cloud.  The same minimums will be maintained between CPEA(Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) and subscription.  Functionally, it will be the exact same as SAP Analytics Cloud public edition sold as subscription.   

 What is the Process for Technical Migration? 

 At any time, a customer can start the new full-use service and use Analytics Content Network to migrate their content from SAC, embedded edition tenants to CPEA SAC, public edition tenants.   You will need to start the SAC, public edition service first, in order to import the content. 

 What Happens to the Existing Customers? 

 During your CPEA contract duration, you’re still able to access the SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition in the SAP BTP cockpit. After your contract ends, the SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition instance will be removed from your sub-account and you won’t be able to provision new service instances. Additionally, you won't be able to access your existing applications, create new ones, nor use the export functionality.

entry.title: Authenticate via Basic Auth or Client Certificate against a BTP Cloud Foundry CAP Application
entry.content:
Hello,

We would like to use the event notification options of C4C to send events directly to a CAP application in Cloud Foundry. Since only Basic Authentication and SSL Client Certificate are available in C4C for event notifications, the question arises whether it is even possible to authenticate to a CAP application via Basic Auth or Client Certificate. Our subaccount is connected to a Custom Identity Provider (Azure AD) and our CAP application uses “XSUAA” as authentication method.

Is there any way to use Basic Auth or Client Certificate to authenticate to a CAP backend service configured with “XSUAA” authentication method?

Many thanks in advance and best regards
Kai-Frederik Engel

entry.title: Re: Display pegged order number in MD04 for the reservation.
entry.content: You could implement BAdI MD_ADD_COL_EZPS to display an additional column containing the order number

entry.title: Cannot unsubscribe from SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In my BTP Subaccount I was trying to delete the SAP Build Process Automation subcription. Unfortunately I get a message saying. "Unsubsription failed".



I already removed any Process Automations in the SAP Build Lobby but I still get the same error.
I found some other questions also with the same error in the community but none that have been resolved with a explanation.
Any help would be appreciated.

Best Regards,

Lukas

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2020 RAP managed key
entry.content:
Hi Sebastian,

 

I was facing the same issue. Did you use the domain XSDUUID_RAW as suggested in the behaviour definition for your UUID property? I replaced it with a self-defined technically identical domain, and it worked.

 

Kind regards

Stefan

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud not all filter elements are displ...
entry.content: I think there is a limitation of results.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud not all filter elements are displ...
entry.content: I think there is a limitation of results. https://me.sap.com/notes/3194682/E

entry.title: Re: How to convert xml to table, using xml transformation, at utf-8 format.
entry.content:
CALL TRANSFORMATION considers that a SOURCE XML variable of type XSTRING (bytes) are characters encoded in UTF-8.

So, I guess your ultimate question is "how to encode characters from STRING to XSTRING in UTF-8"? (question unrelated to CALL TRANSFORMATION)

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 2 (Migration)

entry.title: Announcing BTP Trial Open Hours - Connect with the Product Team!
entry.content:
If you’re exploring the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Trial, we’ve just released something you won’t want to miss – the BTP Trial Open Hours. It’s your chance to connect directly with the trial product team, share your feedback, and ask those burning questions.

What’s BTP Trial Open Hours All About?

It’s simple: You are using the BTP Trial account, and we want to learn about your experiences. During these 1-hour sessions, you get to chat directly with 1-2 trial product team members. It’s an open forum to share what you love about the trial, what could be improved, and where we can do better - all anonymously, of course.

But it’s not just about sharing your thoughts. Got questions? We’ll do our best to help you navigate the platform and make the most of your trial.

Here’s How You Sign Up

To participate in BTP Trial Open Hours, you must have an active BTP Trial account. If not, follow this tutorial to get started. Then, use this direct link to book your session, or alternatively you can access it through the BTP Trial Cockpit:

  1. Log into the BTP Trial Cockpit.
  2. Click "Give Feedback": You’ll see this option in the top right corner. Click on it to get started.
  3. Sign up for BTP Trial Open Hours.

See you around!

Emre

entry.title: Re: How replenishment lead time is getting calculated
entry.content: Same results with scheduling mode 1

entry.title: Re: SAC Add-in for Excel
entry.content: Thanks for the sharing ! 👍

entry.title: Re: ABAP 7.4 Syntax - FOR Loop iteration
entry.content:
Hi @Wanna5 ,
Hope the below code helps

data(lt_spfli_sel) = value ltt_spfli_sel( for <fs_sp> in lt_spfli 
                                          ( VALUE #( BASE CORRESPONDING #( <fs_sp> ) 
                                                                     CARRID = 'FR' ) ) ). 

Thanks.

entry.title: New How-To-Videos: Learn how to make use of the Customer Insights Dashboard
entry.content:
The Customer Insights dashboard in SAP for Me provides a holistic, customizable overview of SAP Solutions across support contracts and deployment types.

We have created short and crisp How-to Videos for you. Just Pick and Choose the ones relevant to you and learn how to get the most out of the functionalities in the Customer Insights dashboard.

1. Getting Started with the Customer Insights Dashboard in SAP for Me

This Video will empower you with the knowledge to make the most of the Customer Insights Dashboard and leverage it to gain invaluable insights into your solution situation. - SAP Video Link

2. Maximising Your Insights with Personalisation in the Customer Insights Dashboard 

With these tips, you can streamline your workflow and create tailored reporting according to your individual needs by using the customisation and personalisation options in the Customer Insights Dashboard. - SAP Video Link

3. Analysing Your System Landscape in the Customer Insights Dashboard

With this video, you'll be able to gain valuable insights into your System landscape, get an overview, or easily find the systems you would like to focus on using the Customer Insights dashboard. - SAP Video Link

4. Closed Cases in the Customer Insights Dashboard 

With this explanation, you'll be able to better understand and optimize your SAP cases using the Customer Insights Dashboard. - SAP Video Link

5. Cloud Availability in the Customer Insights Dashboard 

With these insights, you will be able to utilize the Cloud Availability section to quickly find which systems have been affected by downtime, how critical it was for the SLA, and analyze the availability history across all systems and solutions. - SAP Video Link

To access the new dashboard, make sure your S-user possesses the 'Display Support Situation Reporting' authorisation, which can be provided by your super administrator. For more details kindly refer to this KBA.

Please visit the Customer Insights portal page to stay up-to-date and get all information on access and authorisation, the latest updates, road map, trainings and info sessions, FAQs and more.

SAP for Me is made for you!

Related blogs: SAP for Me goes reporting  or all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting dashboard.

entry.title: How to do a lookup of a field in another model in SAC
entry.content:
From Model 2 the "TYPE" Field has to be taken and filled in the "LOOKUP FIELD" in the Model 1.

Can anyone please let me know how this can be done?

Yes we can have the TYPE as attribute in a dimension table. But just wanted to know how this can be done in the Advanced Formula.

Please forgive me if it is basic question.

entry.title: SAP PI NW7.5 upgrade and split (ctc wizards for PI split)
entry.content:
Hi,

We performed upgrade of SAP dual stack PI NW 7.40 to SAP PI NW 7.5 followed by Split procedure.

We followed upgrade guide, see attached, page 62, 

f the former SAP PI system is a dual-stack implementa­tion, you must carry out after the upgrade of SAP PI to SAP NetWeaver 7.5 the dual-stack-split using the soft­ware provisioning manager for the dual stack-split pro­cedure and the PI Upgrade CTC Wizard for the configu­ration adjustment.

1 - upgrade

2 - split

3 - trying to run PI Upgrade EHP Wizard and it failing.

SAP note DIGITS , see attached, have different sequence: Upgrade, Wizard, Split.

So, 2 documents have different information.

Since Upgrade and split is already done, we are seeking your help on completing PI Upgrade EHP Wizard.

According to the note below, the Wizard cannot be run after split.

Kind regards,

PERSON_NAME.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP PI NW7.5 upgrade and split (ctc wizards for PI sp...
entry.content:
As mentioned in the SAP Documentation: Which CTC Configuration Tasks (Wizards) do I need to execute in my PI/PO systems and when do I execute them? (point 3: Dual Usage upgrade), the correct steps are as follows (see note 2230617 Upgrade and Split of Netweaver Pi Dual Stack in SAP Netweaver 7.5):

  • Upgrade the system to 7.50

  • Execute the Upgrade Wizard e.g. note 1450419 Configuration Wizard: PI Upgrade EHP 

  • Split the ABAP and Java stacks (SUM operation)

  • Execute the Split Wizard as per note 2190371 Configuration Wizard: PI Split 

As per the same note mentioned in the above, to run the CTC Wizard, start the SAP NetWeaver Administrator with "http://<java_host>:<java_port>/nwa" and navigate to "Configuration Management --> Scenarios --> Configuration Wizard". Select "PI Split".

entry.content: Thank you for your answer, it'a SAP MDG AEM BC set /UGI/MDG_EAM_OTC_CODES_920 when I activate it, I get error below and I can not activate data model. I couldn't find anything in SAP Suppot about this issue.

entry.title: Re: Issue in tutorial: Deploy an App to SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime
entry.content:
Thank you for posting the solution.
I had the same issue.

Adding the domain value to the manifest.yml and adding region as described resolved the issue.
I can now also view the deployed instances.

Gave your question and answer a Kudos.

entry.title: SAP IRPA - Your job cannot be distributed because no unattended agent is available
entry.content:
Hello,

I am running the automation through API trigger in VDI (Server system). When I run the API Trigger, the automation job is stuck with the following error: "Your job cannot be distributed because no unattended agent is available" (screenshot attached). Also the agent information is blank (screenshot attached)

- I've made sure the desktop agent is in 'unattended' mode.

But when running the trigger in my Local System, the automation is working fine.

 

Appreciate the community's support in finding a resolution!

SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation

entry.title: Certification exam validity
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Digital skills initiative free certification attempt . Can I give exam within a year but register within 6 months. Or do both within 6 months?

entry.title: GRN is allowed even after Delivery Completed indicator in PO
entry.content:
For purchase orders where no Goods Receipt have been processed or only partial GRs have been recorded, and no additional GRNs are anticipated, the line item is marked as "Delivery Completed."

However, despite marking the Delivery Complete Indicator, the system still allows users to create GRs, which should not occur.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GRN is allowed even after Delivery Completed indicato...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please be noted that, setting the 'delivery completed' indicator has the following effects:

  • The purchase order item is considered as completed and can be deleted even if not the entire quantity was delivered.
  • A further delivery is not expected. This affects material requirements planning (materials planning) and purchase order commitment.
  • However, another delivery up to the purchase order quantity (including overdelivery tolerance) is still possible.

So, even if you set 'Delivery Completed' indicator you could still do the GR.

 

If you want to prevent a GR posting, you can proceed as follows:

  • Reduce the purchase order quantity to the previous GR quantity.
  • Block the purchase order item.
    (ME22N: Block pushbutton; ME22 Menu -> Edit -> Block)
  • Fill the date field for the 'Latest Possible Goods Receipt' (LEWED) in the purchase order item. If you do this and you enter a GR document after this date, the system issues message M7163, which you must set as an error message in Customizing.

Please refer below Note which explains the issue in detail:
856279 - MIGO: Proposed qty despite 'delivery completed' indicator

You can also refer Note 1093582 for the queries related to 'Delivery completed indicator - ELIKZ'

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Done!!

entry.title: Streamline Your PPAP Journey with SAP PLM Collaboration
entry.content:
Introduction: In the intricate landscape of modern manufacturing, quality assurance stands as a cornerstone of success. Ensuring that every component meets stringent standards is not just a goal but a necessity. Enter the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP), a meticulous protocol designed to validate the quality and compliance of supplied production parts before they integrate into your manufacturing processes. In this blog, we delve into how harnessing SAP Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) collaboration can revolutionize your PPAP journey, elevating efficiency, precision, and reliability to unprecedented levels. 

Problem Statement: If SAP PLM Collaboration were not in place, communication with suppliers would be disparate and asymmetric, with less authorization control. Teams would be repeatedly reinventing the process, lacking guardrails and visibility into ongoing, completed, and pending tasks. There would be no logging and tracing of document or information flow, and decisions and approvals would rely on emails or offline modes. The absence of process or project visibility and insights would result in inefficiencies. Document versioning and real-time notifications of changes would be non-existent. 

“Customers have benefitted from SAP PLM Collaboration, enhancing their PPAP process efficiency and optimization across their enterprise. Have you faced similar quality assurance challenges in your PPAP process? Discover how SAP PLM Collaboration can transform your PPAP process and drive efficiency.” 

SAP PLM collaboration provides a centralized platform that resolves these issues by offering streamlined communication, enhanced authorization control, structured workflows, comprehensive visibility into tasks and processes, robust document tracking, and real-time notifications, thereby optimizing the PPAP process and ensuring efficient quality assurance. 

Some abbreviations that will be used in this blog: 
PPAP: Production Part Approval Process  
FFF: Fit, Form, and Function  
PSW: Part Submission Warrant  
QIR: Quality Info Record  
OEM: Original Equipment Manufacturer  
APQP: Advanced Product Quality Planning  
PLM: Product Lifecycle Management

 

Understanding Your PPAP Journey: A High-Level Process Map 

At the heart of your quest for excellence lies a structured and meticulously orchestrated PPAP journey. Let's navigate through its key phases: 

Phase 1: Triggering PPAP The journey commences with the generation of a PPAP request, emanating from your ERP systems. This triggers a cascade of actions, setting the stage for a seamless execution. 

Phase 2: Evaluation Upon receipt of the PPAP request, your teams dive into meticulous evaluation, laying the groundwork for collaborative efforts that follow. This phase ensures alignment with project objectives and your business expectations. 

Phase 3: PPAP Preparation Attention turns to meticulous preparation, from creating registrations to assessing item risk levels and establishing Quality Info Records (QIR). Every detail is scrutinized and curated to uphold the highest standards. 

Phase 4: Automation Leveraging SAP PLM collaboration, workflows are automated based on predefined templates, streamlining processes, and enhancing efficiency. The collaboration workflow template encompasses critical stages such as planning, defining quality scopes, product and engineering design reviews, drawing and specification assessments, and supplier qualifications. Moreover, the collaboration document structure encompasses considerations, team details, benchmarks, goals, KPIs, and compliances, ensuring a holistic approach to quality assurance. 

Phase 5: Making Parts/Docs Collaborative efforts come to fruition as PPAP documents are added by suppliers using your integrated SAP PLM Collaboration platform. Suppliers and OEMs exchange product and other data via SAP PLM Collaboration, ensuring seamless communication and data exchange. Sample PPAP parts are produced, and documents are submitted for review, ensuring adherence to specifications. 

Phase 6: Closure or Reinitiating PPAP With approvals in hand, suppliers ship goods, followed by final processes such as FFF and PSW. Quality Info Records are updated, marking the successful closure of the PPAP cycle. 

Process Visibility and Continuous Improvement These processes are meticulously measured via the Process Visibility dashboard available in your SAP PLM Collaboration platform. This dashboard provides insights into the process, identifying impediments and areas for improvement. By leveraging these insights, you strive to deliver timely, quality products while continuously enhancing efficiency and effectiveness. your SAP PLM platform integrates custom PPAP dashboards, offering stakeholders dynamic visualization tools for real-time insights and actionable intelligence. 

Transformative Outcomes: Complex Custom Workflows and Risk Templates The culmination of your efforts yields transformative outcomes, marked by the emergence of complex custom workflows and risk templates. These tailored solutions empower stakeholders to navigate the PPAP journey with confidence, driving efficiency and upholding stringent quality standards. 

To Summarize: Elevating Quality Assurance with SAP PLM Collaboration In the intricate world of modern manufacturing, quality assurance is essential. Ensuring every component meets strict standards is not just a goal but a necessity. The Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) is a meticulous protocol that validates the quality and compliance of supplied parts before they enter your manufacturing process. This blog explores how SAP Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) collaboration can revolutionize your PPAP journey, boosting efficiency, precision, and reliability. 

Sample Workflow for PPAP stage gates:

Reference: Modeling Workflows 

Sample Process Visibility Dashboard for PPAP Process:  

Sample Collaboration for PPAP process:

Reference: Collaboration  

Further: 
If you're interested in learning more about SAP PLM collaboration solutions, check out our website: https://www.sap.com/products/scm/product-lifecycle-management.html

If you're already a user of SAP PLM collaboration, join our community to stay up to date: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/product-lifecycle-management

About the author: 
Ayan Pandey (ayan.pandey@sap.com), Product Manager for SAP PLM Cloud, specializes in customer-centric innovations and process optimization.

entry.title: Re: Moving average Price History in s4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

You can use the Manage Material Valuations (F2680) app to view the price history for a material:

  1. Enter your material and choose "Go".
  2. Mark the material in the results list and choose "Material Price Analysis".
  3. Under "View" switch to "Price History" in the dropdown menu.

Is this what you were looking for?

Kind regards,

Jessica

entry.title: Quota arrangement for FG From DC to Plants
entry.content:
My client's requirement is like this 

To distribute an MRP-run forecast for finished goods (FG) products from a distribution centre (DC) to three different plants in percentages.

Example Scenario:

  • Distribution Center (DC): 1,000 units forecasted.
  • Plant A: 50% allocation → 500 units
  • Plant B: 30% allocation → 300 units
  • Plant C: 20% allocation → 200 units
How do you map this in SAP using a Quota arrangement? Do we need to create vendor master data for plants? Please help in detail for set up.
 
Thanks 
Raviram

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud IAG - Issues and Resolutions
entry.content:
Hello @Raffaella,

Could you verify if the "USER_ID" application parameter is set to "LOGIN_NAME" in the IAG "Configuration" tile?

Afterwards, please locate the user in IAS who is experiencing issues with access request submission. Ensure that their user ID is correctly entered in the "Login Name" field in "User Management". This ID should correspond with the one in the target system.

Run the Repository Sync job for the IAS application to sync this change in user data from IAS to IAG and then try creating the access request again.

Hope it should resolve your issue. Thanks!

entry.content:

This app is developed in s4 1809 version and I am trying to import it from s4 2023 version ( we have upgraded recently ).

Please help in resolving the issue.
